title: Space Tech Briefing — June 29, 2026
summary:
Good morning, space enthusiasts! Welcome to your daily dose of cosmic wonder, coming to you live on this incredible Monday, June 29, 2026. What a morning it is shaping up to be!

We're kicking things off with truly breathtaking news from the European Space Agency! Today, the European spatial telescope Euclid has just unveiled the heart of our very own Milky Way galaxy like never, ever before. This is monumental! Euclid has delivered the largest, highest-resolution image ever captured of the Milky Way's center, all in stunning visible light. Just imagine the detail!

And to truly appreciate the scale of this achievement, let's take a moment to dive deeper into this incredible mosaic. As we reported last week, on Thursday, June 24th, the European Space Agency first teased us with this spectacular view. This isn't just a photo; it's a colossal achievement: a gigantic 6-gigapixel mosaic obtained by the Euclid telescope. It reveals over 60 million stars in a single, breathtaking scene, offering an unprecedented level of detail in visible light across one of the densest regions of our galaxy.

Euclid diligently acquired this data back in March 2025 during a marathon 26-hour observation session. The telescope precisely aimed its camera at the galactic bulge, that bright central region of the Milky Way, meticulously stitching together nine distinct shots. Each individual pointing covered a portion of the sky larger than a full Moon! And for those vibrant, true-to-life colors that bring the cosmos to life, the teams brilliantly complemented Euclid's visible data with contributions from the ground-based Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope. It's a true testament to international collaboration and cutting-edge science, giving us a front-row seat to the very core of our galaxy!

Absolutely incredible stuff! That's all for your space technology briefing this morning. Stay curious, keep looking up, and join us again tomorrow for more cosmic updates. From all of us here, have a fantastic day!
